hypothesis: H3 static sequencer beats handshake lowering

wrapper_behavior: reuses one tile kernel across four output phases
forms the upper weight address bits from phase_q
forms the upper output store address bits from phase_q
auto-acks tile output for phases 0..2 and exposes out0_valid only on phase 3

decision: Static wrapper replacement is not the next H3 lever; the existing wrapper is already static and adds negligible mapped cost relative to the generated tile kernel.
next_h3_target: A bounded static tile-kernel proof that removes generated handshake buffers/forks/muxes inside the 64x64 kernel while preserving the external activation/weight/store contract.
